Title: A Comprehensive Multiomics Approach Illuminates the Biosynthetic Mechanism of Scoparone in Artemisia capillaris

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major bioactive polymethoxylated coumarin; reported in the paper as associated with treatment of jaundice, liver inflammation and cholecystitis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: flowers and leaves of Artemisia capillaris, highest in flowers
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: scoparone biosynthesis within the phenylpropanoid/coumarin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terminal O-methylation step of coumarin/scoparone branch
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type I OMT/COMT-like; Methyltransf_2 domain and dimerisation domain; conserved plant OMT motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: scopoletin; isoscopoletin; SAM as methyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: methylates scopoletin and isoscopoletin to form scoparone as the final biosynthetic step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as key terminal/last committed step; rate limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes scoparone biosynthesis and accumulation; coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana enabled de novo scoparone production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in flowers, followed by leaves; minimal in stems and roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA-metabolite correlation; coexpression analysis; phylogenetic analysis; recombinant protein enzyme assays in E. coli; HPLC metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; kinetics and substrate-binding assays; molecular docking and MD simulation; transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcOMT1 was identified from the scoparone-associated coexpression module and correlated with scopoletin, isoscopoletin and scoparone accumulation. 2. Recombinant AcOMT1, but not AcOMT2 or AcOMT3, converted scopoletin or isoscopoletin into scoparone in vitro. 3. AcOMT1 is cytoplasmic and its expression pattern matches scoparone accumulation across tissues. 4. Kinetic, binding, docking and MD analyses showed higher affinity and catalytic preference for scopoletin over isoscopoletin. 5. Together with AtF6'H1 and AtCOSY, AcOMT1 reconstituted scoparone biosynthesis in Nicotiana benthamiana, yielding 3.03 microg g^-1 DW.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: for transient production in Nicotiana benthamiana, plants were grown under greenhouse conditions at 25 degrees C with 16 h light/8 h dark and subjected to extended light exposure for 2 days after infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and full-length transcriptomics; NCBI SRA BioProject PRJNA1164833; metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S CaMV promoter, constitutive, in Nicotiana benthamiana transient expression construct
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AcOMT1 is multifunctional because it accepts both scopoletin and isoscopoletin, but shows stronger affinity and catalytic efficiency toward scopoletin; the paper presents it as the missing final methylation step in scoparone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: prime target for metabolic engineering or breeding of Artemisia capillaris with increased scoparone; useful for heterologous scoparone production and could be stacked with upstream pathway genes and flux-enhancing regulators

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates feruloyl-CoA at the 6' position to provide precursor for coumarin/scopoletin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: described as crucial gene responsible for scopoletin biosynthesis in the heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required with AtCOSY and AcOMT1 to enable de novo scoparone production in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: confirmed only in transgene-expressing Nicotiana benthamiana leaves by RT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtF6'H1 was selected as a crucial upstream scopoletin biosynthetic gene for pathway reconstruction. 2. Coexpression of AtF6'H1 with AtCOSY and AcOMT1 generated scoparone in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves. 3. Control leaves expressing GFP alone did not produce detectable scoparone or intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: extended light exposure for 2 days after agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a heterologous upstream enzyme from Arabidopsis, not discovered in Artemisia capillaris in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful as an upstream module to stack with AcOMT1 and COSY for heterologous scoparone production

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes coumarin ring formation in the scopoletin biosynthetic route that feeds scoparone formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: described as crucial gene responsible for scopoletin biosynthesis in the heterologous reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required with AtF6'H1 and AcOMT1 to enable de novo scoparone production in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: confirmed only in transgene-expressing Nicotiana benthamiana leaves by RT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; RT-PCR; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtCOSY was used as a crucial upstream scopoletin biosynthetic gene in the reconstituted pathway. 2. Coexpression with AtF6'H1 and AcOMT1 yielded scoparone in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves. 3. GFP control leaves lacked detectable scoparone or intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: extended light exposure for 2 days after agroinfiltration in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a heterologous upstream enzyme from Arabidopsis, not identified from Artemisia capillaris omics screening in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful pathway-stacking component for heterologous and synthetic biology production of scoparone
